mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 2.0% and continues climbing, the cost of capital increases, reducing the present value of future corporate earnings. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days as equity valuations compress in response to tighter monetary conditions.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.68% on 2022-07-01 with recent tightening cycle underway
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 2.0% and rising, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in total nonfarm payroll employment signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity. This mechanism operates as an always-on trend signal predicting negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations are revised downward.
failure_condition: Payroll employment declines for 3+ consecutive months yet SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of such periods, or correlation between PAYEMS momentum and 60-day SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales (RSAFS) decline month-over-month while simultaneously housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1,500 thousand units, this signals a synchronized contraction in consumer and residential demand. The joint weakness indicates recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines month-over-month AND HOUST < 1500 thousand
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 680,591 million and HOUST at 1,559 thousand on 2022-06-01
failure_condition: The joint condition (RSAFS decline + HOUST < 1500) is triggered in 10+ instances yet SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of those instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) rising above 105.0 (2017=100) signals robust supply-side capacity utilization and manufacturing strength. This mechanism operates as a threshold-triggered always-on signal predicting positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as earnings surprises from strong production data flow through equity markets.
failure_condition: INDPRO exceeds 105.0 in 15+ instances yet SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of those instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises above 295.0 (1982-1984=100) while the federal funds rate remains below 2.5%, real rates are negative, signaling monetary accommodation despite high inflation. This interaction produces positive equity returns over 20 trading days as investors seek inflation hedges in equities.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 295.271 and FEDFUNDS at 1.68% on 2022-07-01
failure_condition: The joint condition (CPIAUCSL > 295 + FEDFUNDS < 2.5%) occurs in 8+ instances yet SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of those instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 4.0%, labor market slack increases, reducing wage pressure and corporate cost burdens. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as profit margins expand and recession fears moderate.
failure_condition: UNRATE exceeds 4.0% in 12+ instances yet SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of those instances, or the correlation between UNRATE level and subsequent 60-day returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) declining quarter-over-quarter signals weakening aggregate demand and economic contraction. This always-on trend mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings forecasts are revised downward in response to slowing growth.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 19,681.682 billion (vintage) on 2022-04-01 with significant revision risk
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 declines in 8+ instances yet SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of those instances, or the correlation between GDPC1 growth rate and subsequent 60-day returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production (INDPRO) falls below 102.0 while unemployment (UNRATE) rises above 3.8%, this signals simultaneous supply weakness and labor market deterioration, indicating stagflationary pressure. The joint condition produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as equity valuations compress under earnings and growth uncertainty.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 104.3648 and UNRATE at 3.5% on 2022-06-01 and 2022-07-01
failure_condition: The joint condition (INDPRO < 102 + UNRATE > 3.8%) is triggered in 10+ instances yet SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of those instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).
